quick_wins: Delete 292 stale branches across 96 projects — zero risk, instant cleanup
Add justification for 2 projects with ≤5 unjustified false positives each — quick audit fix

ai_insights.narrative: 64 repositories have not had a Sonar scan for more than one year. Of these, 49 have not been scanned for over two years, and 30 have not been scanned for over four years. The most material hygiene risks in this portfolio of 210 repositories are concentrated in inactive projects, extensive stale non-main branches across 92 repositories, and scanner configuration gaps affecting 23 repositories. Quality gate compliance is strong, with no non-compliant gates detected; however, 14 quality profile findings indicate that several built-in and custom profiles require attention to stay current.

sections.inactive_insights: 64 repositories have not had a Sonar scan for more than one year. Of these, 49 have not been scanned for over two years, and 30 have not been scanned for over four years. These stale projects increase noise in reports and represent unnecessary maintenance overhead.
sections.inactive_action: Request project POCs to review the 64 inactive repositories for continued relevance, with inactive ones to be removed or archived from the Sonar instance to optimise cost and maintain a clear project portfolio.
sections.duplication_insights: 2 repositories exceed the defined duplication threshold, indicating a higher level of repeated code.
sections.duplication_action: Review these 2 repositories with project POCs to identify and reduce duplication, and improve code efficiency and maintainability.
sections.branch_repos_non_standard_main_branch_in: 1 repository is set up in Sonar to scan a branch other than the expected main branch. This can lead to confusion and make reports less consistent across projects.
sections.branch_repos_non_standard_main_branch_ac: Review this repository with the project POC to confirm the correct branch is set for scanning, and update it where needed for consistency.
sections.branch_repos_with_inactive_non_main_bran: 92 repositories have non-main branches such as feature or development branches that have not been scanned for more than 180 days, which may indicate stale or abandoned work.
Review these repositories with project POCs to confirm if the branches are still needed, and remove or clean them up to maintain a well-managed Sonar environment.
sections.branch_repos_with_non_main_branch_count_: 11 repositories have more non-main branches than the agreed threshold, which can clutter scan results and make reporting harder to follow.
Review these 11 repositories with project POCs to consolidate or remove surplus non-main branches and keep the branch count within the agreed threshold.
sections.false_positives_insights: 6 repositories have issues marked as false positives across the portfolio. Of these, 2 repositories have false-positive markings recorded without justification comments, weakening auditability.
sections.false_positives_action: Review the false-positive markings in the 2 repositories that lack justification comments and add comments so all suppression decisions remain auditable.
sections.suppressions_insights: 3 repositories have accepted issues recorded without justification comments, making it difficult to assess whether the suppressions are valid and reducing auditability.
sections.suppressions_action: Add justification comments to the accepted issues in the 3 repositories so suppression decisions remain auditable.
sections.quality_gates_insights: All 210 repositories use a compliant Quality Gate, ensuring consistent quality assurance across the portfolio.
sections.quality_gates_action: No action needed; all repositories are compliant with the required Quality Gate configuration.
sections.config_gaps_insights: 23 repositories have scanner configuration gaps that suppress metric collection, reducing visibility into code quality for a significant portion of the portfolio.
sections.config_gaps_action: Add the missing scanner configuration to the 23 repositories to restore metric visibility and ensure accurate quality reporting.
sections.sonarqube_rules_insights: 14 quality profile findings have been identified across the portfolio. 10 built-in profiles covering key languages including Java (87 projects), HTML (39 projects), JavaScript (38 projects), and CSS (34 projects) have not been updated for over 857 days, with JSON, PL/SQL, and XML profiles last updated over 1,064 days ago. Additionally, 4 custom profiles do not inherit from a built-in profile, requiring manual updates to stay current with rule changes.
sections.sonarqube_rules_action: Review and update the 10 stale built-in language profiles to ensure rules are current and projects benefit from the latest analysis improvements; update the 4 custom profiles to inherit from their respective built-in profiles, or document the rationale for keeping them standalone.
